step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{B}
Clinical reasoning: After a total thyroidectomy, there is a significant risk of developing hypoparathyroidism due to accidental injury or removal of the parathyroid glands. Hypoparathyroidism leads to decreased production of parathyroid hormone (PTH), resulting in hypocalcemia. Symptoms of hypocalcemia include muscle spasms (such as hand cramps observed during BP measurement), tetany, and perioral numbness. Hypocalcemia causes increased neuromuscular excitability and can lead to prolonged QT intervals on electrocardiogram (ECG) due to changes in cardiac membrane stability. Among the given choices, QT prolongation is the most characteristic ECG change seen in hypocalcemia.
